title: Paneion bulbosum
taxonomy: Paneion bulbosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Asphodeloideae, which includes plants commonly known as asphodels. The genus Paneion is part of the tribe Asphodelae, which consists of plants that are typically found in dry, rocky areas. Paneion bulbosum is native to the Mediterranean region, where it grows in well-drained soils. Its classification is closely related to other asphodels, which are often characterized by their succulent leaves and showy, white or yellow flowers.

intro: Paneion bulbosum, also known as Bulbous Paneion or Paneion, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Paneion bulbosum include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
